meaning: adj.肖像的,肖像学的,图像材料的,图示法的象征
examples: This, in turn, made different iconographic demands on painting.
The aggregation of all volumes defines its external iconographic value.
In addition, an exclamation point is often used as an iconographic symbol to express importance.
Elsewhere, as in an untitled canvas of 1941-42, the deconstructed and reorganized Crucifixion imagery suggests the terrifying power of familiar iconographic bits and pieces.
